title: merremia tridentata subsp. genuina
intro: Merremia tridentata subsp. genuina is a subspecies of the Merremia tridentata plant, commonly known as the 'thorny passionflower' or 'devil's shoestring'. It is a perennial climber that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Merremia tridentata subsp. genuina include its ability to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat various skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne. In herbal preparations, it can be used as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and stems can be dried and made into a powder for use in various remedies.
